---
title: "cyto-assist Tutorial"
author: "Cytognosis Team"
format:
  html:
    theme:
      light: [cosmo, custom.scss]
      dark: [darkly, custom.scss]
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: show
    code-tools: true
    smooth-scroll: true
jupyter: python3
---

# Introduction

Welcome to the interactive cyto-assist tutorial.
This document highlights the *Cytognosis* methodology, mapping high-dimensional health datasets leveraging rocm scale compute.

```{python}
import cyto_assist as pkg

# Check version mapping
print(f"Running cyto-assist version {pkg.__version__}")
```

## System Requirements

This execution relies on the prelocked PyTorch bindings injected heavily by the underlying `uv` environment mapping.

```{python}
#| message: false
#| warning: false
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import torch

print(f"PyTorch Version: {torch.__version__}")
print(f"Is CUDA/ROCm available? {torch.cuda.is_available()}")
```

## The Insight

Instead of analyzing biological noise univariately, we build cellular intelligence platforms resolving holistic patterns.

```{python}
# Create benchmark synthetic data
shape_size = 1000
data = np.random.randn(shape_size, shape_size)

# Basic matrix validation
res = data @ data.T
print(f"Computed reference projection of shape {res.shape}")
```

## Conclusion

This Quarto template natively adapts to Cytognosis branding elements and is evaluated during the `nox -s quarto_build` pipeline.
> "Before diagnosis. Before symptoms. There's Cytognosis."
